title: George CPF does it again
description: GEORGE NEWS - It was a proud moment for...
bodyText: [{"type":"contributor","text":"Marguerite van Ginkel","contributorName":"Marguerite van Ginkel","contributorDate":"Friday, 30 January 2026, 12:00"},{"type":"intro_bold","text":"Pretorius commended the CPF committee, neighbourhood watch structures and all other role players for their exceptional teamwork and professionalism."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"GEORGE NEWS - It was a proud moment for Rupert Pretorius, chairperson of the George CPF, when it was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards for the 2024-2025 period. The awards ceremony took place in Cape Town on Saturday 17 January.","html":"GEORGE NEWS - It was a proud moment for Rupert Pretorius, chairperson of the George CPF, when it was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards for the 2024-2025 period. The awards ceremony took place in Cape Town on Saturday 17 January."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Pretorius commended the CPF committee, neighbourhood watch structures and all other role players for their exceptional teamwork and professionalism.","html":"Pretorius commended the CPF committee, neighbourhood watch structures and all other role players for their exceptional teamwork and professionalism."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"He noted that the Western Cape has 151 police stations and that the ratings were measured against standard CPF criteria. He said, however, what set George apart from its peers were the initiatives that are not always reflected in the formal assessment. These include joint operations with the police, investigative support, and the effective use of licence plate recognition (LPR) systems and CCTV.","html":"He noted that the Western Cape has 151 police stations and that the ratings were measured against standard CPF criteria. He said, however, what set George apart from its peers were the initiatives that are not always reflected in the formal assessment. These include joint operations with the police, investigative support, and the effective use of licence plate recognition (LPR) systems and CCTV."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Pretorius further said that the provincial police commissioner, Lieutenant General Thembisile Patekile, the Minister of Com-munity Safety, Anroux Marais, and the Provincial Head of CPFs, Fransina Lukas, along with several other officials, expressed their deep appreciation for the George CPF's efforts, which consistently go above and beyond expectations.","html":"Pretorius further said that the provincial police commissioner, Lieutenant General Thembisile Patekile, the Minister of Com-munity Safety, Anroux Marais, and the Provincial Head of CPFs, Fransina Lukas, along with several other officials, expressed their deep appreciation for the George CPF's efforts, which consistently go above and beyond expectations."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"George CPF was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards 2024/25. Photo: Rupert Pretorius","imageUrl":"https://cms.groupeditors.com/img/5cb16de1-5896-448c-964c-69ae92504912.jpeg","caption":"George CPF was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards 2024/25. Photo: Rupert Pretorius"}]

These include joint operations with the police, investigative support, and the effective use of licence plate recognition (LPR) systems and CCTV.","html":"He noted that the Western Cape has 151 police stations and that the ratings were measured against standard CPF criteria. He said, however, what set George apart from its peers were the initiatives that are not always reflected in the formal assessment. These include joint operations with the police, investigative support, and the effective use of licence plate recognition (LPR) systems and CCTV."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Pretorius further said that the provincial police commissioner, Lieutenant General Thembisile Patekile, the Minister of Com-munity Safety, Anroux Marais, and the Provincial Head of CPFs, Fransina Lukas, along with several other officials, expressed their deep appreciation for the George CPF's efforts, which consistently go above and beyond expectations.","html":"Pretorius further said that the provincial police commissioner, Lieutenant General Thembisile Patekile, the Minister of Com-munity Safety, Anroux Marais, and the Provincial Head of CPFs, Fransina Lukas, along with several other officials, expressed their deep appreciation for the George CPF's efforts, which consistently go above and beyond expectations."},{"type":"image_with_caption","text":"George CPF was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards 2024/25. images.caption: George CPF was awarded third place overall at the annual Provincial Prestige Excellence Awards 2024/25. Photo: Rupert Pretorius

author: Marguerite van Ginkel
teaser: Pretorius commended the CPF committee, neighbourhood watch structures and all other role players for their exceptional teamwork and professionalism.
featuredCaption: Excellence celebrated: Constable Alshandre Louw (2nd runner-up, man of the year), Sergeant Charl Botha (sportsman of the year), Rupert Pretorius (CPF George chairperson, receiving the price on behalf of the CPF), Anthony Noble (speaker and attendee), Colonel Lionel Kennedy (George Police's acting station commander), Warrant Officer Wendy Lee (woman of the year) and Captain Deon Floris (2nd runner-up, sportsman of the year).
